ExpandedDescription: Custom ServiceNow widgets and layouts frequently misuse heading levels (e.g., multiple H2s without H1 or skipping from H1 to H3), violating WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships. This breaks screen reader navigation and document structure for users relying on headings for orientation.
AcceptanceCriteria: All pages and widgets have correct, logical heading hierarchy starting with one H1 per page and no skipped levels.
Test: Run axe-core or WAVE on multiple pages; confirm no heading structure violations. Use screen reader heading navigation to verify logical flow.
Solution1: Add proper heading hierarchy comments and examples in core-overrides widget templates
Solution2: CSS rule to enforce visual heading styles consistent with semantic levels
Solution3: a11y.scss block with role=heading and aria-level enforcement
Solution4: Theme-level layout template standardization for headings

ExpandedDescription: Widgets with card-like structures use divs styled as headings without proper <h*> tags, violating WCAG 1.3.1 and making content unnavigable for screen reader users.
AcceptanceCriteria: Every visual heading uses the correct semantic <h1>-<h6> tag.
Test: Inspect widget cards in litmus test; confirm semantic headings with screen reader.
Solution1: Replace styled divs with proper h-tags in widget HTML notes in core-overrides
Solution2: CSS class mapping for visual headings to semantic ones
Solution4: Portal widget audit and template update

ExpandedDescription: Dynamic content sections (AJAX loaded) introduce new headings out of order or duplicate H1s, violating WCAG 1.3.1.
AcceptanceCriteria: Dynamic updates maintain overall page heading hierarchy.
Test: Trigger dynamic load in litmus widget and re-check heading structure with axe.
Solution1: JS polyfill to adjust heading levels on dynamic content
Solution3: a11y.scss with aria-live for structure changes

ExpandedDescription: Sidebar or secondary navigation uses H2/H3 incorrectly relative to main content, breaking document outline.
AcceptanceCriteria: Sidebar navigation uses appropriate heading levels relative to main content.
Test: Navigate sidebar and main content with screen reader heading list; confirm logical order.
Solution1: Template adjustments in core-overrides for sidebar headings

ExpandedDescription: Knowledge article or catalog descriptions misuse bold text as fake headings without semantic tags.
AcceptanceCriteria: All visual headings are semantic <h*> elements.
Test: Check knowledge articles and catalog items with axe-core; zero fake heading violations.
Solution1: CSS rule to convert common fake headings to real ones via content

ExpandedDescription: Modal dialogs contain headings that conflict with page-level headings.
AcceptanceCriteria: Modals have their own contained heading hierarchy starting with H2 or H3.
Test: Open modal and check heading structure with screen reader; confirm no conflict.
Solution1: Scoped heading rules in core-overrides for modals

ExpandedDescription: Dashboard widget titles use inconsistent heading levels across different widgets.
AcceptanceCriteria: All dashboard widgets use consistent H2 or H3 for titles.
Test: Scan dashboard with axe; confirm uniform heading levels.
Solution1: Global widget title template in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Tabbed interfaces have tab panels without proper heading inside.
AcceptanceCriteria: Each tab panel starts with appropriate heading.
Test: Switch tabs and verify heading navigation works.
Solution1: Tab panel template updates

ExpandedDescription: List items or cards use H4/H5 incorrectly as primary content headings.
AcceptanceCriteria: Heading levels match content importance.
Test: Check list and card layouts with screen reader.
Solution1: Template fixes in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Search results pages have multiple H1s or missing structure.
AcceptanceCriteria: Search results page has single H1 and logical sub-headings.
Test: Perform search and check structure with axe.
Solution1: Search widget template in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Animations and transitions in ServiceNow portals (carousels, modals, hover effects, loading spinners) do not respect @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), violating WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide. Users with vestibular disorders experience discomfort.
AcceptanceCriteria: All animations respect reduced-motion preference and can be paused/stopped.
Test: Emulate reduced motion in DevTools and interact with animated elements; confirm no motion.
Solution1: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { * { transition: none !important; animation: none !important; } } in snowboots-a11y.scss
Solution3: JS polyfill to detect and disable animations

ExpandedDescription: Carousels auto-advance without pause/stop controls or reduced-motion respect, violating WCAG 2.2.2.
AcceptanceCriteria: Carousels have pause/play controls and respect reduced motion.
Test: Load carousel in litmus widget; confirm pause works and reduced motion stops auto-play.
Solution1: Override carousel JS with pause controls in core

ExpandedDescription: Page load or widget load animations are distracting and cannot be paused, violating WCAG 2.2.2.
AcceptanceCriteria: All load animations are short (<5s) or respect reduced motion.
Test: Reload page and observe animations with reduced motion enabled.
Solution1: CSS animation control in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Tooltips appear only on hover and disappear immediately, violating WCAG 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus for keyboard users.
AcceptanceCriteria: Tooltips are accessible via focus and persist until dismissed.
Test: Focus on elements with tooltips using keyboard; confirm tooltip stays visible.
Solution1: CSS :focus-visible for tooltips in core

ExpandedDescription: Session or form timeouts occur without 20-second warning, violating WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
AcceptanceCriteria: Users receive clear warning before timeout and can extend time.
Test: Simulate timeout scenario; confirm warning appears.
Solution1: JS timeout warning polyfill

ExpandedDescription: Content does not reflow properly at 400% zoom, causing horizontal scroll and loss of information, violating WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow.
AcceptanceCriteria: Page remains usable without horizontal scrolling at 400% zoom.
Test: Zoom browser to 400% and navigate; confirm no horizontal scroll.
Solution1: CSS max-width and flex improvements in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Some text elements have fixed pixel sizes that do not scale with user font settings, violating WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text.
AcceptanceCriteria: All text resizes up to 200% without loss of content.
Test: Change browser font size; confirm all text scales.
Solution1: Use rem/em units everywhere in core-overrides

ExpandedDescription: Drag-and-drop interfaces (e.g. in kanban or file uploads) have no keyboard equivalent, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.
AcceptanceCriteria: Drag-and-drop functionality has full keyboard alternative.
Test: Test drag-and-drop with keyboard only; confirm equivalent actions.
Solution1: JS keyboard drag-and-drop polyfill
Solution2: CSS focus styles for draggable items

ExpandedDescription: Hover-activated content (menus, tooltips) disappears when mouse moves, violating WCAG 1.4.13.
AcceptanceCriteria: Hover content remains visible until dismissed or focused away.
Test: Hover over elements; confirm content stays.
Solution1: CSS :hover + :focus-visible persistence

ExpandedDescription: Complex parallax or spinning animations in dashboards cause motion sickness for some users, with no reduced-motion option.
AcceptanceCriteria: All motion-heavy elements respect prefers-reduced-motion.
Test: Enable reduced motion and reload dashboard; confirm no triggering animations.
Solution1: Global reduced-motion rule in a11y.scss
